first_paragraph: ***Annie Oakley*** is an American [Western television series](/wiki/Westerns_on_television "Westerns on television") that fictionalizes the life of the famous [Annie Oakley](/wiki/Annie_Oakley "Annie Oakley"). (Except for depicting the protagonist as a phenomenal [sharpshooter](/wiki/Sharpshooter "Sharpshooter") of the period, the program entirely ignores the facts of the historical Oakley's life.)[*[original research?](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research "Wikipedia:No original research")*] Featuring actress [Gail Davis](/wiki/Gail_Davis "Gail Davis") in the title role, the weekly program ran from January 1954 to February 1957 in [syndication](/wiki/Television_syndication "Television syndication"). A total of 81 black-and-white episodes were produced, with each installment running 25 minutes in length. [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") aired daytime [reruns](/wiki/Rerun "Rerun") of the series on Saturdays and Sundays from 1959 to 1960 and then again from 1964 to 1965.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Western Show

| *Annie Oakley* | |
| --- | --- |

***Annie Oakley*** is an American [Western television series](/wiki/Westerns_on_television "Westerns on television") that fictionalizes the life of the famous [Annie Oakley](/wiki/Annie_Oakley "Annie Oakley"). (Except for depicting the protagonist as a phenomenal [sharpshooter](/wiki/Sharpshooter "Sharpshooter") of the period, the program entirely ignores the facts of the historical Oakley's life.)[*[original research?](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research "Wikipedia:No original research")*] Featuring actress [Gail Davis](/wiki/Gail_Davis "Gail Davis") in the title role, the weekly program ran from January 1954 to February 1957 in [syndication](/wiki/Television_syndication "Television syndication"). A total of 81 black-and-white episodes were produced, with each installment running 25 minutes in length. [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") aired daytime [reruns](/wiki/Rerun "Rerun") of the series on Saturdays and Sundays from 1959 to 1960 and then again from 1964 to 1965.

## Synopsis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Synopsis")]

The series starred [Gail Davis](/wiki/Gail_Davis "Gail Davis") as Annie Oakley, with [Brad Johnson](/wiki/Brad_Johnson_(actor,_born_1924) "Brad Johnson (actor, born 1924)") as Deputy Sheriff Lofty Craig and [Jimmy Hawkins](/wiki/Jimmy_Hawkins "Jimmy Hawkins") as Annie's little brother, Tagg Oakley; Hawkins appeared in 80 of the series' 81 episodes. In the [pilot](/wiki/Television_pilot "Television pilot") episode, "Bull's Eye", Tagg is played by [Billy Gray](/wiki/Billy_Gray_(actor) "Billy Gray (actor)") (better known for his role as James "Bud" Anderson, Jr. on the TV version of *[Father Knows Best](/wiki/Father_Knows_Best "Father Knows Best")*).[[1]](#cite_note-etvs-1) After playing Tagg in the *Annie Oakley* pilot, Gray joined the cast of *Father Knows Best* (which premiered in October 1954, nine months after the initial broadcast of *Annie Oakley*).

In the series, Annie Oakley rides a horse named Target: Tagg's horse is Pixie, and Lofty's mount is Forest.[[2]](#cite_note-Brooks_and_Marsh-2) Annie and Tagg live in the fictional town of Diablo, [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona") with their uncle, Sheriff Luke MacTavish, who is frequently away when trouble starts; it is then up to straight-shooting Annie and her "silent suitor", Lofty Craig, to rescue law-abiding neighbors and arrest outlaws.[[2]](#cite_note-Brooks_and_Marsh-2) Tagg is often told to stay in town and out of the way. When the need arises to relay important new information or capture by outlaws, he usually ends up in the middle of each episode's adventure.

Annie usually wears the same fringed cowgirl outfit, of which 15 (or more) copies were made during the show's production.[[3]](#cite_note-3) She wears her hair in braided [pigtails](/wiki/Pigtail "Pigtail"). Additional episodes were considered, but Hawkins had a growth spurt and outgrew his role as Annie's little brother.[[4]](#cite_note-4) *Annie Oakley* was one of [Gene Autry](/wiki/Gene_Autry "Gene Autry")'s Flying A Productions.

## Guest stars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Guest stars")]

[Dick Tufeld](/wiki/Dick_Tufeld "Dick Tufeld") appeared fifteen times in assorted roles during the course of the series; [Harry Lauter](/wiki/Harry_Lauter "Harry Lauter"), twelve times; [Stanley Andrews](/wiki/Stanley_Andrews "Stanley Andrews") and [Gregg Barton](/wiki/Gregg_Barton "Gregg Barton"), eleven times each; [Myron Healey](/wiki/Myron_Healey "Myron Healey"), ten times; [Roy Barcroft](/wiki/Roy_Barcroft "Roy Barcroft"), eight times; [William Fawcett](/wiki/William_Fawcett_(actor) "William Fawcett (actor)"), prior to his role as Pete Wilkey in *[Fury](/wiki/Fury_(American_TV_series) "Fury (American TV series)")*, seven times; [William Tannen](/wiki/William_Tannen_(actor) "William Tannen (actor)"), six times, and [Don C. Harvey](/wiki/Don_C._Harvey "Don C. Harvey"), five times.

* [Richard Alexander](/wiki/Richard_Alexander_(actor) "Richard Alexander (actor)") as George Todd in "Annie and the Lacemaker" (1956)
* [Chris Alcaide](/wiki/Chris_Alcaide "Chris Alcaide"), as Paul Dodson in "Annie and the Brass Collar", as Vic in "The Cinder Trail", and as Duke Jaegar in "Outlaw Mesa" (all 1954)
* [Roscoe Ates](/wiki/Roscoe_Ates "Roscoe Ates"), as Curley Dawes in "Showdown at Diablo" (1956) and as Walsh in "Annie and the Miser" (1957)
* [Jim Bannon](/wiki/Jim_Bannon "Jim Bannon"), as Frank Jessup in "Flint and Steel" and as Ben Porter in "Tagg Oakley, Sheriff" (both 1956).
* [John Beradino](/wiki/John_Beradino "John Beradino"), professional baseball player-turned-actor, as Gorman in "Annie Rides the Navajo Trail" and as Roscoe Barnes in "Amateur Outlaw" (both 1956)
* [James Best](/wiki/James_Best "James Best"), as Scott Warren in "Outlaw Mesa" and as Jess Dugan in "Annie and the Outlaw's Son" (both 1954)
* [Monte Blue](/wiki/Monte_Blue "Monte Blue"), as Tom Wheeler in "Flint and Steel" and as Mr. Peabody in "Tagg Oakley, Sheriff" (both 1956)
* [X Brands](/wiki/X_Brands "X Brands"), as Randy in "Annie and the First Phone", as Tenanda in "Indian Justice", and as Peter Maher in "Annie Rings the Bell" (all 1956)
* [Eve Brent](/wiki/Eve_Brent "Eve Brent"), under the name of Jean Lewis, played Clara Bickel, girlfriend of Banker's employee in "Sure Shot Annie" in 1955
* [Lane Chandler](/wiki/Lane_Chandler "Lane Chandler"), appeared in the 1955 episode "Annie Breaks an Alibi" as a lawman
* [Harry Cheshire](/wiki/Harry_Cheshire "Harry Cheshire"), as Windy Smith in "Annie Breaks an Alibi" and as Judge Everard Bowen in "Annie and the Higher Court" (both 1955)
* [Virginia Dale](/wiki/Virginia_Dale "Virginia Dale"), appeared as Mrs Wiggins in "Flint and Steel" in 1956
* [John Doucette](/wiki/John_Doucette "John Doucette"), as Hugo Barrett in "Escape from Diablo" (1954) and as Duke Bailey in "Trouble Shooter" (1955)
* [Lisa Gaye](/wiki/Lisa_Gaye "Lisa Gaye"), appeared as the phony daughter in "Annie and the Lacemaker"
* [Gary Gray](/wiki/Gary_Gray_(actor) "Gary Gray (actor)") as Bucky Donavan in "Annie and the Six o' Spades" (1954)
* [James Griffith](/wiki/James_Griffith "James Griffith"), as George Martin in "Annie Takes a Chance" and as Mark Banning in "Powder Rock Stampede" (both 1955)
* [Ron Hagerthy](/wiki/Ron_Hagerthy "Ron Hagerthy"), as Chuck Hutchins in the title role "The Waco Kid" and as Billy Stryker in "Annie Rings the Bell" (both 1956)
* [I. Stanford Jolley](/wiki/I._Stanford_Jolley "I. Stanford Jolley") as Walt, with [Darryl Hickman](/wiki/Darryl_Hickman "Darryl Hickman") as Chet Sterling, in "Annie Gets Her Man" (1954)
* [Alan Hale, Jr.](/wiki/Alan_Hale,_Jr. "Alan Hale, Jr."), as Eli Gorham in "Annie Calls Her Shots" and as Moose Bosco in "Annie and the Silver Ace" (both 1954)
* [Dick Jones](/wiki/Dick_Jones_(actor) "Dick Jones (actor)"), as Corporal Sam in "Annie Joins the Cavalry", as Bob Neil in "Annie Helps a Drifter", and as Steve Donavan in "Annie and the Six o'Spades" (all 1954) and as Clell Morgan in "Annie and the Junior Pioneers" (1955)
* [L. Q. Jones](/wiki/L._Q._Jones "L. Q. Jones") portrayed Cal Upton in "The Robin Hood Kid" and Ned Blane in "Dilemma at Diablo" (both 1956)
* [Douglas Kennedy](/wiki/Douglas_Kennedy_(actor) "Douglas Kennedy (actor)") appeared as Jim Hayward in "Annie Takes a Chance" and as Ralph Putnam in "Powder Rock Stampede" (both 1955).
* [Harry Lauter](/wiki/Harry_Lauter "Harry Lauter") played Will Davis in "The Reckless Press" (1956). A saddle tramp who is jailed for suspected murder.
* [Nan Leslie](/wiki/Nan_Leslie "Nan Leslie") played Alias Annie in "Alias Annie Oakley" (1954). The episode also stars [Lane Bradford](/wiki/Lane_Bradford "Lane Bradford") as Malcolm and [Harry Lauter](/wiki/Harry_Lauter "Harry Lauter") and Johnson.
* [Keye Luke](/wiki/Keye_Luke "Keye Luke") appeared as Li Wong, a [Chinese](/wiki/China "China") laundryman and landowner, in the 1955 episode "Annie and the Chinese Puzzle", which touches on racial discrimination.
* [Tyler MacDuff](/wiki/Tyler_MacDuff "Tyler MacDuff") appeared in four episodes in 1956, including the title guest-starring role in "The Saga of Clement O'Toole" and as Don "Doc" Briggs in "Dilemma at Diablo".
* [Tyler McVey](/wiki/Tyler_McVey "Tyler McVey") appeared in the 1956 episode "Showdown at Diablo" in the role of Senator Ridgeway.
* [Eve Miller](/wiki/Eve_Miller "Eve Miller") appeared in the 1955 episode "Sure Shot Annie" in the role of Jane Lester. She appears to play a somewhat dubious Southern belle.She also appeared in the 1956 episode "The Reckless Press" as Kathy Stokes. Kathy marries a newspaper editor, but only after a tragedy and a near- tragedy.
* [Ewing Mitchell](/wiki/Ewing_Mitchell "Ewing Mitchell"), as Ab Forsyth in "Annie and the Twisted Trails" and Major Paley in "Santa Claus Wears a Gun" (both 1956) and Colonel Granger in "Dude's Decision" (1957)
* [Fess Parker](/wiki/Fess_Parker "Fess Parker"), as newspaper publisher Tom Conrad in "Annie and the Mystery Woman" and as Les Clinton in "Annie and the Texas Sandman" (both 1954)
* [George Pembroke](/w/index.php?title=George_Pembroke&action=edit&redlink=1 "George Pembroke (page does not exist)"), played The Welshman in "Sharpshooting Annie" in 1954
* [William Phipps](/wiki/William_Phipps_(actor) "William Phipps (actor)"), as George Wessel in "Sure Shot Annie (1955), as Dan Carter in "Annie and the Twisted Trails" (1956), and as Earl Wallace in "Dude's Decision (1957)
* [Slim Pickens](/wiki/Slim_Pickens "Slim Pickens"), as Sundown in "Annie and the Leprechauns", as Slim in "The Waco Kid", as Ed Morgan in "Annie Rings the Bell", and as Garner in "Grubstake Bank" (all 1956)
* [Denver Pyle](/wiki/Denver_Pyle "Denver Pyle"), as Dr. Barnes in "Valley of the Shadows" and as Tom Malloy in "Annie's Desert Adventure" (both 1954)
* [Mike Ragan](/w/index.php?title=Mike_Ragan&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mike Ragan (page does not exist)"), four episodes, "The Runaways" (1954), "Thunder Hill" as henchman Walt Newman (1955), "Annie Rides the Navajo Trail" as Sgt. Yorkton (1956), and "Amateur Outlaw" (1956)
* [Arthur Space](/wiki/Arthur_Space "Arthur Space"), as Carl Bishop in "Valley of the Shadows" and as The Warden in "Annie's Desert Adventure" (both 1954)
* [Glenn Strange](/wiki/Glenn_Strange "Glenn Strange"), as Idaho in "Outlaw Brand" and as Ernie Barker in "Treasure Map" (both 1956)
* [Lyle Talbot](/wiki/Lyle_Talbot "Lyle Talbot"), as Pa Wiggins in "Flint and Steel" and as Colonel Dawson in "Tagg Oakley, Sheriff" (both 1956)
* [Lee Van Cleef](/wiki/Lee_Van_Cleef "Lee Van Cleef") appeared as Tim Brennan in "Annie Breaks an Alibi" and as Amos Belcher in "Annie and the Higher Court" (both 1955).
* [John War Eagle](/wiki/John_War_Eagle "John War Eagle") played Thunder Cloud in the 1956 episode "Indian Justice".
* [Pierre Watkin](/wiki/Pierre_Watkin "Pierre Watkin"), as Henry Lormier in "Dean Man's Bluff" (1955) and as the Reverend Mills in "The Reckless Press" (1956)
* [Carleton G. Young](/wiki/Carleton_G._Young "Carleton G. Young"), as the Sheriff in "Sundown Stage" (1956) and as Colonel Jackson in "The Dutch Gunmaker" (1957)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Release")]

### Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Home media")]

Approximately two dozen episodes lapsed into the public domain and are currently available on DVD in North America (including several releases issued through Davis's estate) and elsewhere.

On March 31, 2009, Mill Creek Entertainment released *Gun Justice Featuring The Lone Ranger*, with other television Westerns such as *Annie Oakley*.

[VCI Entertainment](/wiki/Viva_Entertainment "Viva Entertainment") released the complete series on DVD in Region 1 for the first time on October 21, 2014.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Media")]

### Comics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Comics")]

A number of American comics were based on the TV series:

* *Annie Oakley and Tagg* 4-18 (1953–1959) by [Dell Comics](/wiki/Dell_Comics "Dell Comics")
* *Annie Oakley and Tagg* 1 (1965) by [Gold Key Comics](/wiki/Gold_Key_Comics "Gold Key Comics")

Many of these issues were later reprinted in black and white by L. Miller and World Distributors Ltd.

### Books

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Annie_Oakley_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Books")]

At least three "Authorized TV Adventures" were published by Whitman Publishing Company.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* *Annie Oakley in Danger at Diablo*, by Doris Schroeder, 1955
* *Annie Oakley in Double Trouble*, by Doris Schroeder, 1958
* *Annie Oakley in Ghost Town Secret*, by Doris Schroeder, 1957
